originalTitle: Democrats call for investigation into ICE officer shooting in Maine after new reports emerge
summary: Lawmakers press for review of hiring practices amid a spate of ICE-related deaths within the same week
content: Democratic lawmakers are pressing for a review of Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) hiring practices and continuing calls for an investigation into the circumstances surrounding the killing of an immigrant in Maine by one of the agency’s officers after news media reported allegations of past violent and threatening behavior by the officer, according to his family members. The Associated Press, the Portland Press Herald and National Public Radio (NPR) all identified the officer in question as David Brouillette, information that the publications attributed to his family. The outlets also reported allegations that Brouillette had a history of mental health issues and had purportedly subjected his ex-wife to violent, threatening behavior. The Guardian has not been able to independently verify the allegations or confirm Brouillette’s involvement in the 13 July shooting death in Biddeford, Maine, of Joan Sebastián Durán Guerrero. The Department of Homeland Security, which oversees ICE, has not identified Brouillette. Messages left at telephone numbers associated with him were not immediately returned. Nonetheless, Bennie Thompson, a Mississippi congressman and the top Democrat on the US House’s homeland security committee, told the AP in a statement that the officer’s alleged violent history “directly call[s] into question the supposed vetting and training ICE does of its recruits”. The exact circumstances of Guerrero’s killing remained unclear on Friday. But the death of the 25-year-old Colombian man has drawn fresh criticism of ICE in large part because only days earlier, another ICE officer fatally shot Lorenzo Salgado Araujo in Texas. In addition to the killings of Guerrero and Salgado, there were two other subsequent, ICE-related deaths within the same week that also led the public and lawmakers to call for independent investigations of DHS, which has been carrying out the White House’s aggressive anti-immigrant campaign. There had also been several other people shot to death by federal immigration officers dating back to the start of Donald Trump’s second presidency in early 2025. “This senseless tragedy must be investigated, and the officer responsible should be taken off our streets and face justice for his actions,” Thompson added in his statement. In response to the AP’s reporting about Brouillette, top US Senate Democrat Chuck Schumer similarly criticized the Trump administration for its mass hiring of thousands of ICE officers to help carry out its mass deportation goals. Schumer, of New York, accused the Trump administration of rushing 12,000 agents “onto our streets without ensuring they were fit to carry a badge and a gun”. The president and his Republican allies then “gave this rogue agency vast power and no accountability”, Schumer continued on X, arguing that neither Trump nor his party can “disown the deadly consequences” of that approach by ICE. The AP reported that it obtained a voicemail that Brouillete was alleged to have left his ex-wife, Ashley, in late 2025, around the time he joined ICE. “Do I think that you should have your … throat cuts or should have had them cut?” says the voicemail, a recording of which NPR aired on Friday. “Yep.” Brouillette was first identified as the alleged shooter in the Guerrero case by the Portland Press Herald. Then the AP and NPR released reports of their own with Brouillette’s identity. Brouillette was reported to be a military veteran and former police officer before being hired by ICE in 2025. A day after the deadly shooting, the Atlantic reported that the officer who killed Guerrero had been recently hired by the agency. DHS spokesperson Lauren Bis said to the AP: “We will never confirm or deny attempts to dox our law enforcement officers.” But Bis claimed the officer in question has nearly a decade of federal law enforcement experience. Guerrero’s survivors include a three-year-old daughter and the child’s mother, his partner.
chineseTitle: 新报告出现后，民主党人呼吁对缅因州 ICE 官员枪击事件进行调查
chineseSummary: 由于同一周内发生大量与 ICE 相关的死亡事件，立法者敦促对招聘做法进行审查
chineseContent: 据一名移民和海关执法局 (ICE) 的家人称，在新闻媒体报道了该官员过去的暴力和威胁行为的指控后，民主党议员正在敦促对移民和海关执法局 (ICE) 的招聘做法进行审查，并继续呼吁对该机构一名官员在缅因州杀害一名移民的情况进行调查。美联社、波特兰新闻先驱报和国家公共广播电台 (NPR) 均确认涉案警官为大卫·布鲁耶特 (David Brouillette)，这些出版物将这一信息归咎于他的家人。这些媒体还报道称，布鲁耶特有心理健康问题史，据称曾对他的前妻实施暴力、威胁行为。 《卫报》尚未能够独立核实这些指控，也无法确认布鲁耶特是否参与了 ​​7 月 13 日缅因州比德福德发生的琼·塞巴斯蒂安·杜兰·格雷罗 (Joan Sebastián Durán Guerrero) 枪击事件。负责监管 ICE 的国土安全部尚未确认布鲁耶特的身份。在与他相关的电话号码上留下的信息没有立即得到回复。尽管如此，密西西比州国会议员、美国众议院国土安全委员会民主党领袖本尼·汤普森在一份声明中告诉美联社，该警官涉嫌的暴力历史“直接让人质疑 ICE 对其新兵进行的所谓审查和培训”。格雷罗被杀的具体情况周五仍不清楚。但这名 25 岁哥伦比亚男子的死亡引发了对 ICE 的新批评，很大程度上是因为就在几天前，另一名 ICE 官员在德克萨斯州枪杀了洛伦佐·萨尔加多·阿劳霍 (Lorenzo Salgado Araujo)。除了格雷罗和萨尔加多被杀之外，同一周内还发生了另外两起与 ICE 有关的死亡事件，
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于全球新闻信号，适合用于观察外部环境、政策变化、地缘风险和市场情绪。
